
# Let's make it Demo Ready – Web UI & User Experience

## Goal
Deliver a smooth, colourful, and accessible Tetris game playable in any modern web browser, with a focus on friendliness, inclusion, and trust.

## Scope
- Web-based UI (HTML/CSS/JS, served via Python)
- Smooth block movement and keyboard controls (arrows or WASD)
- Clear, colourful board and distinct shapes
- Score and level display
- Friendly messages for line clears and game over
- Accessibility: keyboard-only, screen reader support, colour-friendly
- Easy start: one command, instant browser link

## Functional Requirements
- Render a Tetris board in the browser
- Support all Tetromino shapes with smooth falling and stacking
- Allow keyboard controls for movement and rotation
- Display score and level, updating as you play
- Detect and clear filled lines, show friendly feedback
- Increase speed as levels progress, but keep gameplay fair
- Show clear game over and allow easy restart

## System Behaviour
- Game starts in browser after running a single command
- All controls and instructions visible on screen
- No installation or setup required beyond running the command
- Works in Codespaces and on local machines

## Constraints
- No browser plugins or extra installs
- Must work with keyboard only (no mouse required)
- Colour palette must be accessible
- All messages must be clear, friendly, and inclusive

## Non-Goals
- No advanced animations or sound effects
- No multiplayer or online features

## Audience
Everyone, including non-technical users and those using assistive technology. The game must be welcoming and easy to use.

## Success Criteria
- Game is playable and enjoyable in any browser
- Controls and rules are clear and visible
- Accessibility features are present and effective
- Game is easy to start and share
- Players trust the system and feel included
